understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game is to complete every level by placing selectable pieces onto goal tiles (marked '+'). All goal tiles must be occupied simultaneously to trigger a WIN and advance to the next level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement is grid-based (3 pixels per cell) with MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN actions. Some objects are pushable blocks and some are immovable walls. The 'movement.{direction}_blocked' heuristics are not always accurate—actual blocking behavior must be discovered through action. Multiple selectable pieces exist, and CLICK with grid/pixel coordinates switches control to another piece, but only if the click coordinates exactly match a piece's position. The piece I controlled got stuck or was unable to reach goals, and repeated CLICK attempts failed because either the target coordinates were wrong or pieces were blocked/overlapping in a way that prevented switching. The level likely required a specific sequence of pushes and piece switches that I did not discover, leading to exhaustion of the step budget.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game is to complete every level by placing selectable pieces onto goal tiles (marked '+'). All goal tiles must be occupied simultaneously to trigger a WIN and advance to the next level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement is grid-based (3 pixels per cell) with MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN actions. Some objects are pushable blocks and some are immovable walls. The 'movement.{direction}_blocked' heuristics are not always accurate—actual blocking behavior must be discovered through action. Multiple selectable pieces exist, and CLICK with grid/pixel coordinates switches control to another piece, but only if the click coordinates exactly match a piece's position. The piece I controlled got stuck or was unable to reach goals, and repeated CLICK attempts failed because either the target coordinates were wrong or pieces were blocked/overlapping in a way that prevented switching. The level likely required a specific sequence of pushes and piece switches that I did not discover, leading to exhaustion of the step budget.
